stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England — and the whole Philly camp shifts
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles shipped their star to the Patriots, and that one trade reshuffles two whole outfits. Up in New England, Brown's a No. 1 again and Drake Maye just got himself a real gunslinger — mark both up. But the diamond in the rough? DeVonta Smith. With Brown gone he's been drawin' near 29% of the throws his way (his 'cut of the throws,' in plain English), puttin' up 75-plus yards every game without him. Go stake that claim before the saloon wakes up. — Doc, your Fabled AI NFL Trail Guide
stories.source: came over the telegraph line this morning from the CBS Sports desk

stories.headline: Achane laid up at minicamp — don't spook just yet
stories.read: Hold up there before you toss a top-five back off the wagon. Miami's De'Von Achane is nursin' a busted wing — a shoulder, the city analysts say — and he's been kept to individual reps in mandatory minicamp. But every sign points to him bein' saddled and ready come trainin' camp. That means a nervous leaguemate might let him slide a hair in the great land grab. If he dips, you pounce — that's cheap gold on a fella who handles the reins twenty times a game.
stories.source: somebody from RotoWire called this one in on one of Alex Bell's new doohickies

stories.headline: Kyler Murray takes the reins in Minnesota — and that's good news for Jefferson
stories.read: The Vikings call it an open showdown, but most everybody inside the ranch knows Kyler Murray's the bet to start over J.J. McCarthy in '26. Why's that matter to your roster? Justin Jefferson, pardner. A live-armed veteran slingin' it beats breakin' in a greenhorn — Jefferson's ceiling floods higher with Murray under center. McCarthy's the wagon that may throw a wheel in dynasty leagues, so handle that share careful.
stories.source: a rider brought this down from the ESPN outpost
